channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NBC La Riba service boosts financial inclusion for Muslims across Tanzania
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: The National Bank of Commerce (NBC) has been making big moves in improving access to banking for Tanzanians, especially for Muslims, through its interest-free La Riba banking service. O...

item.title: Iran war disrupts global oil supply and drives up energy prices amid Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran, now in its third week as of mid-March 2026, has severely impacted the global energy market through what experts describe as 'energy warfare.' Iran has effectively blockaded th...

item.title: Oscar predictions, cultural recommendations and stories of resilient women
item.description: This article offers a roundup of cultural highlights ahead of the Oscars weekend. The author reflects on the theatrical nature of awards shows after watching Seth Rogen’s satire The Studio, and shares...

item.title: Kenya records 62 deaths as heavy floods displace thousands
item.description: Heavy rains and floods have caused severe destruction in Kenya, especially in the capital, Nairobi. According to police, a total of 62 people, including eight children, have lost their lives since the...

item.title: First Large U.S. Offshore Wind Farm Completed After Legal and Weather Delays
item.description: America’s first large-scale offshore wind energy project has officially completed construction, marking a major milestone for renewable energy in the United States. According to WBUR, the $4.5 billion...

item.title: Eric Maxim Choupo-Moting Secures Late Draw for Red Bulls Against Toronto FC
item.description: In a tense MLS matchup on Saturday, Red Bull New York salvaged a 1-1 draw against Toronto FC at BMO Field, thanks to veteran Eric Maxim Choupo-Moting. The 36-year-old striker, coming on in the 77th mi...

item.title: Theme Parties: How Fans and Brands Are Creating Immersive Experiences
item.description: Theme parties have emerged as a prominent feature of the modern social scene, transforming ordinary gatherings into immersive experiences. Unlike regular house parties, these events revolve around a c...

item.title: Teen Australian sprinter Gout Gout wins 200m at Queensland Championships
item.description: At the Queensland Athletics Championships in Brisbane, teen sprint sensation Gout Gout clinched the open-age 200-metre title in a gripping finish. Despite strong competition from Rory Easton, who push...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Sunny Spells and Scattered Rain Across Several Counties on March 15
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (Kenya Met) has issued the weather forecast for Sunday, March 15, highlighting mostly sunny conditions across the country with localized rainfall in certain countie...

item.title: Broken Brand Promises: The Reality Behind Rewards for Filipino Olympic Champions
item.description: The recent discussion around Hidilyn Diaz’s supposed 'lifetime free fuel' offer highlights a recurring issue in Philippine sports: broken or misleading corporate promises to athletes. While the Zamboa...

item.title: Timothée Chalamet’s Intensive Table Tennis Training for Marty Supreme
item.description: Timothée Chalamet devoted several years to perfecting his table tennis skills for his role as Marty Mauser in the film Marty Supreme, earning a best actor nomination at the 2026 Oscars. Chalamet began...

item.title: PTI urges Pakistan to lead emergency OIC talks on escalating Iran conflict
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has expressed serious concern over the fast worsening security situation in the Middle East, following the US and Israeli attacks on Iran. The party has u...

item.title: South Africa faces potential fuel shortages as refinery capacity declines
item.description: South Africa’s fuel supply is coming under increasing pressure amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which could disrupt global oil flows. Experts warn that the country’s reliance on i...

item.title: Cyprus’ Civil Defence Faces Urgent Reform After Drone Strike Alerts Highlight Shelter Shortages
item.description: On 2 March, Valentinos Pangalos, a senior officer of Cyprus’ civil defence, activated sirens at Paphos International Airport after a suspicious drone, believed to be explosive-laden, approached the fa...

item.title: Volvo EX30 EV Destroyed in Johannesburg Fire Amid Global Battery Recall
item.description: A Volvo EX30 electric vehicle caught alight and was completely destroyed in Bedfordview, Johannesburg, on Thursday afternoon. Fortunately, no one was injured in the incident. This fire is believed to ...

item.title: Professor Haastrup suggests artificial intelligence solution for Nigeria’s power challenges
item.description: Professor Adetunji Haastrup, a Nigerian public analyst and international council member at Harvard University Publishing, don talk say the country fit use artificial intelligence to fix the wahala of ...

item.title: Finance Ministry staff reminded to uphold honesty and teamwork in daily duties
item.description: DODOMA: Finance Minister Khamis Mussa Omar has called on all staff at the Ministry of Finance to always act with integrity and uphold ethical conduct in their work. He emphasized that ethics and hones...

item.title: LPG Shortage Prompts Chennai eateries to explore sustainable cooking options
item.description: A severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders has hit restaurants, bakeries, and tea stalls in Chennai hard, forcing many to rethink their cooking methods. The crisis stems from disruptions in imports...

item.title: George Ruto Spends Time with Nairobi Biker Crew on Dirt Bike
item.description: George Ruto, the lastborn son of President William Ruto, has been spotted mingling with Nairobi's popular biker crew, 12 O' Clock Boyz. The young pilot, who is also skilled in flying helicopters, arri...

item.title: Pakistan Conducts Military Strikes in Kandahar Against Taliban and Terrorist Facilities
item.description: Pakistan military carried out overnight airstrikes in Kandahar, Afghanistan, targeting sites linked to Afghan Taliban and TTP-affiliated terrorist groups, according to state media. The operation, name...
